reasoning: While North Korea has maintained technical readiness for a seventh nuclear test since 2022, and March 2025 US intelligence reports specifically warned of readiness 'on short notice,' the actual likelihood in a narrow two-month window (May-June 2025) remains low. Major holidays in April 2025 passed without a test, and recent North Korean military activity and rhetoric have focused on diversifying delivery systems (naval and missiles) rather than an imminent underground detonation. Given the 7.5-year hiatus since the last test and the lack of specific imminent indicators in April 2025, the probability is set at 8%.
